news.summary: Lisa Sanchez has been appointed as the new Vice President of Software Development at Assurity in Lincoln, Nebraska, effective April 14, 2025. She brings a wealth of knowledge and experience to the role, aiming to spearhead innovative initiatives. Sanchez's main objective is to create enhanced processes and tools that simplify the purchasing process, ultimately benefiting the company.
news.headline: Assurity appoints Lisa Sanchez as VP of Software Development to drive innovations, streamlined processes for easier buying.

news.summary: In Hopewell, Pennsylvania on April 14, 2025, Lincoln Learning Solutions revealed its receipt of the International Society for Technology in Education Seal. This award highlights the company's dedication to providing top-tier, evidence-based digital learning solutions.
news.headline: Lincoln Learning Solutions receives ISTE Seal for Excellence in Digital Learning, showing dedication to research-based solutions.

news.summary: BORDENTOWN, New Jersey - On April 14, 2025, TIGRIS Aquatic Services, LLC (TIGRIS), a prominent aquatic management and environmental services provider, revealed its acquisition of LakePro, Inc., a well-regarded aquatic services firm in Swartz Creek, Michigan. This strategic move aims to bolster TIGRIS’s industry presence.
news.headline: TIGRIS Aquatic Services, LLC acquires LakePro, Inc. expanding into Michigan. Acquisition of Swartz Creek-based company. (Characters: 104)

news.summary: Certara, Inc. (NASDAQ: CERT) announced its anticipated financial performance for the first quarter, expecting a revenue of $106 million. This marks a 10% increase from the previous year's $96.7 million and surpasses the consensus of $104.42 million. Software revenue is predicted to reach $46.4 million, a growth of 18%, while services revenue is projected to be $59.6 million, a 4% rise. Bookings for Q1 are expected to reach $118 million, showing a 12% increase, with software bookings at $40.6 million and services bookings at $77.4 million. Additionally, Chemaxon revenue and bookings are forecasted to be $5.9 million and $4.9 million, respectively. The company also anticipates an adjusted EBITDA in the range of $33-$35 million.
news.headline: Certara plans $100M buyback, with expected Q1 revenue at $106M (up 10%) and bookings at $118M (up 12%). Software revenue: $46.4M (up 18%), services revenue: $59.6M (up 4%). Adjusted EBITDA expected at $33-$35M.

news.summary: Bitcoin bulls are attempting to push the price over $85,000 as Michael Saylor's firm buys 3,459 Bitcoin for $285.5 million, boosting their total holding to 531,644 BTC at an average price of $67,556. However, institutional investors have trimmed holdings, with digital asset ETPs seeing $795 million in outflows last week and $7.2 billion since February, reversing year-to-date inflows to $165 million. Bitcoin seems to have bottomed out in the short term, but a strong bull market rally isn't expected soon. The S&P 500 Index could see range-bound action between 5,500 and 5,119. The US Dollar Index is in a downtrend, while Bitcoin could climb to $89,000 but may face resistance there. Ether is facing selling pressure at $1,722, and XRP is seeing resistance at $2.24. BNB faces resistance at the downtrend line, and Solana is at a crucial level at the 50-day SMA ($130). Dogecoin may rally to $0.20 if it breaks above moving averages. Cardano struggles to move above $0.65, while UNUS SED LEO faces resistance near $9.39. Every investment move carries risks, and readers should do their own research before investing.
news.headline: BTC price targets $89k after $85k purchase by Michael Saylor's Strategy. US Dollar Index hits 97.50 support level. ETH faces resistance at $1,722. XRP at $2.24. BNB resists downtrend line. SOL close to $147 - $153. DOGE recovery faces bear resistance. ADA struggles at $0.65. LEO range between $9.90 and $8.79 seen. Market outlook suggests cautious optimism.

news.summary: Philadelphia, April 14, 2025 - PowerPay, a top financial tech platform, has successfully issued an investment-grade bond with KeyBank as the exclusive placement agent. This move will enhance PowerPay's balance sheet and provide a significant boost to their financial position.

news.summary: In WOBURN, Massachusetts, on April 14, 2025, ZwitterCo released a new series of sanitary superfiltration (SF) spiral membrane elements utilizing their advanced second-generation SF technology. These membranes are FDA approved for whey processing.

news.summary: The SEC has delayed a decision on approving Ether staking in two Grayscale funds, the Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF and Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF, until June 1, with a final deadline set for the end of October. Staking would allow investors to lock up their ETH in wallets to support blockchain operations and earn rewards, potentially offering a yield of 2.4% on Coinbase and between 2%-7% on Kraken. Since their launch in 2024, Ether ETFs have seen a cumulative net inflow of $2.28 billion, with other asset managers like BlackRock's 21Shares iShares Ethereum Trust also vying for staking permissions. The SEC has approved options trading for spot Ether ETFs, enhancing utility for institutional investors while also highlighting Ether's struggles compared to Bitcoin ETFs, which have seen higher net flows. Additionally, Ether has underperformed assets like XRP and Solana during the current bull market, with trading below $2,000 as of April 14.
news.headline: SEC delays decision on Grayscale ETH ETF staking, now set for June 1. ETH staking offers 2-7% yield, ETFs attract $2.28B inflow since 2024 launch.

news.summary: Nvidia revealed intentions to manufacture AI supercomputers in the United States alongside its collaborators. On Monday, Nvidia's stock remained relatively stable. The move marks a strategic step by the company to establish a manufacturing base that will produce advanced artificial intelligence technology within the U.S. This initiative is expected to bring substantial economic benefits and promote technological advancements in the region.
news.headline: Nvidia to produce AI supercomputers in the U.S. with partners; stock unaffected.

news.summary: On Monday, President Donald Trump announced plans to provide assistance to certain car companies amid his implementation of 25% tariffs on imported goods. As a result, stock prices for General Motors and Ford experienced an increase. However, Tesla did not benefit from this news.
news.headline: Trump's 25% tariffs boost GM and Ford stocks; Tesla left out.

news.summary: An Italian wine importer to the US, Empson, known for his innovative work, received a lifetime achievement award in the wine industry. The honor was posthumously given to company founder Neil Empson by Empson USA. The announcement was made in Alexandria, Va., on April 14, 2025. Empson's significant contributions to the industry were recognized through the prestigious 'Vinitaly' award.
news.headline: Neil Empson received 'Vinitaly International Award' posthumously for lifetime contributions to wine industry at 57th Vinitaly.

news.summary: In Red Bank, NJ on April 14, 2025, VeeMost Technologies Inc. (OTC: VMST), a prominent provider of innovative IT solutions, has received approval from the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) for its name change from Global Developments, Inc. to VeeMost Technologies Inc. The Company's ticker symbol has been changed from GDVM to VMST. These modifications were featured on the OTC Markets Daily List on April 14, 2025, and will become effective at the commencement of business on April 15, 2025.
news.headline: VeeMost Technologies Inc changes name from Global Developments Inc, ticker symbol from GDVM to VMST approved by FINRA.

news.summary: Can-Fite BioPharma Ltd., based in Ramat Gan, Israel, revealed through a press release that they have finalized deals for the sale of 2,500,000 of their American Depositary Shares (ADSs) at a rate of $1.20 per ADS in a registered direct offering. This move, aimed at advancing their proprietary small-molecule drugs for oncological and inflammatory diseases, is anticipated to be completed on or around April 15, 2025, pending the fulfillment of standard closing prerequisites. The company’s stocks are listed on the NYSE American under the ticker symbol CANF, as well as on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange (TASE) under the same symbol.
news.headline: Can-Fite to offer 2.5M ADS at $1.20 each, raising $3.0M; to advance drugs for oncology and inflammatory diseases.

news.summary: Last week, there was a significant outflow of funds from products tracking Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies due to the uncertainty surrounding Donald Trump's fluctuating tariffs. However, XRP funds saw an exception as they experienced inflows during the same period.
news.headline: Investors withdrew $789M from Bitcoin, Ethereum funds last week due to Trump's tariff uncertainty, except for XRP funds.

news.summary: Parking brake actuators are essential safety components and represent one of the rapidly expanding segments in the industry. The Standard® Parking Brake Actuators stand out as they are completely new products, not remanufactured, and are engineered to deliver original equipment performance. These actuators are designed to smoothly integrate with electronic systems, ensuring optimal functionality.

news.summary: In Naples, Florida on April 14, 2025, Music Licensing, Inc. (OTC: SONG) and its subsidiary Pro Music Rights, Inc. (PMR) have responded to the U.S. Copyright Office’s Notice of Inquiry (Docket No. 2025–1). The response highlights anti-competitive actions by traditional Performing Rights Organizations (PROs) like BMI and ASCAP. PMR's submission showcases an alternative licensing approach that is equitable.

news.summary: On April 14, 2025 in Los Angeles, ETAF announced a competitive emergency grant cycle initiative in light of the current Administration's decisions causing funding gaps for essential domestic HIV programs. The initiative aims to assist organizations facing financial challenges by offering grants. This measure is crucial to support the affected programs and ensure continued provision of vital services in the fight against HIV.

news.summary: On April 14, 2025, in Pittsburgh, an individual with chronic back spasms and pain expressed the difficulty of performing exercises like bench presses. The person highlighted the need for a solution to allow for lower and upper back exercises without experiencing pain.

news.summary: In Louisville, Kentucky, on April 14, 2025, a new dental implant center has opened, offering a solution for those seeking quick dental care. After 20 years of "Teeth-in-a-day®", this new facility now provides permanent teeth in just 24 hours. The Nuvia Dental Implant Center is introducing this revolutionary service, ending the long wait for patients in need of swift dental solutions.
news.headline: Nuvia Dental Implant Center opens in Louisville, offering permanent teeth in 24 hours after two decades of Teeth-in-a-day®.

news.summary: A recent announcement from St. Petersburg reveals that Colony Grill has renewed its partnership with the Tampa Bay Rowdies, extending their collaboration for four more years. As the Official Pizza of the team, Colony Grill will continue to offer fans 8-inch personal pizzas at Al Lang Stadium. Supporters will have the option to select from a variety of three different pizzas during matches. This extension follows a fruitful collaboration in 2024 and solidifies the presence of Colony Grill at the stadium for the foreseeable future.
news.headline: Colony Grill renews 4-year deal as Official Pizza of Tampa Bay Rowdies, serving 8-inch personal pizzas at Al Lang Stadium.

news.summary: Stable coin issuers, resembling banks they aim to disrupt, have 70-90% of deposits in fixed income instruments. Sensitive to interest rates, inflation, and AUM changes, stable coins face risks like banks, like Silicon Valley Bank in 2023. The 1933 banking crisis led to FDIC creation to insure deposits. Glass–Steagall Banking Act of 1933 aimed to de-risk banks by prioritizing client interests in deposits. Broker/dealer fintechs, like stable coin issuers, lack regulations and deposit insurance. They rely on AUMs and bond markets, now unstable with 10-Year Treasury bonds seeing a spike in yields. Safe havens aren't guaranteed even with high-grade bonds. Stable coin issuers lack safety nets, unlike banks under FDIC protection.
news.headline: Stable coins backed by fixed income investments face risks from market fluctuations, needing regulation for stability. Bond yields spiked, raising concerns.

news.summary: In Chicago on April 14, 2025, Shapiro+Raj, a top Strategic and Innovative Insights Consultancy, has announced the addition of four new members to its life sciences team. This move aims to bolster its position as a leading provider of forward-thinking insights to clients. Shapiro+Raj currently ranks as the number 1 Strategic and 12th Most Innovative Consultancy in the industry.
news.headline: Shapiro+Raj in Chicago welcomes 4 new hires to its life sciences team, enhancing its innovative strategies. #12 Most Innovative.

news.summary: A recent report highlighted that the global population has exceeded 7.9 billion people, with more than 4.66 billion individuals having access to the internet. This data underscores the growing connectivity and digital dependence across the world.
